description: Re-anchor the completed AICodeWith provider harness to the follow-up goal of exposing only live-verified AICodeWith models in `/model list`.
steps: Record in `PROGRESS.md` that the user asked for live verification and `/model list` catalog exposure after the provider integration landed.
Replace `PLAN.json` so the active harness tracks the catalog-update follow-up instead of the already-completed provider-routing plan.
Preserve the existing note about the unrelated `tests/test_coding_mode.py` baseline failure as a repository caveat rather than reclassifying it as part of this task.

description: AICodeWith candidate models are validated against the user's current local API key before any new `/model list` entries are added.
steps: Use the current local `providers.aicodewith` config from `~/.nanobot/config.json` to probe the candidate model set.
Record which models return a successful minimal response and which ones fail upstream.
Limit the new catalog entries to the models that were actually verified as usable in this run.

description: The provider catalog exposes curated AICodeWith entries in `/model list` even though AICodeWith is implemented as a gateway.
steps: Update `nanobot/providers/catalog.py` so `aicodewith` is not dropped by the blanket gateway skip rule.
Keep the existing gateway-skip behavior for other providers unless they are explicitly allowlisted.
Verify the catalog still avoids adding speculative entries for unvalidated gateways.

description: The AICodeWith catalog entries match the live-verified usable model set rather than the generic OpenAI or Anthropic defaults.
steps: Add the verified AICodeWith GPT/Codex, Claude, and Gemini model names to the curated provider catalog.
Do not include models that returned live upstream errors in this run.
Verify the model strings match the forms expected by nanobot model switching.

description: Model-list tests prove AICodeWith now contributes curated catalog entries and only for the intended verified models.
steps: Replace the old assertion that AICodeWith must not produce catalog entries.
Assert that the verified AICodeWith catalog models appear with `provider_name == aicodewith` and `source == catalog`.
Keep the test isolated from network calls by validating the catalog output shape rather than replaying live probes.

description: The `/model list` follow-up is checkpointed with targeted tests and progress notes so later sessions can trust the visible catalog entries.
steps: Run the focused pytest commands that cover the updated catalog behavior.
Append the live-verification results and the targeted test results to `PROGRESS.md`.
Commit the checkpoint after the catalog update passes verification.
